title: Update moved in Calendar View
excerpt: Has anyone else noticed the updates icon has moved to behind a 3-dot menu when using the calendar view? It is super inconvenient because our workflow is dependent on team members easily knowing when there are updates on …

posts.author: Kristi.TD (Kristi TD)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone else noticed the updates icon has moved to behind a 3-dot menu when using the calendar view? It is super inconvenient because our workflow is dependent on team members easily knowing when there are updates on an item. Now they have to click through on every item which is super inconvenient and has already caused us to have to do double work.

posts.author: LGH (Laura Habberstad)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. This change adds another step to our process. I enjoyed having the ability to see that update notification in a single glance at the ticket.

posts.author: HeatherLarson (Heather )
posts.cookedHtml:
Kristi TD:

Has anyone else noticed the updates icon has moved to behind a 3-dot menu when using the calendar view? It is super inconvenient because our workflow is dependent on team members easily knowing when there are updates on an item. Now they have to click through on every item which is super inconvenient and has already caused us to have to do double work.

Kristi TD:

Has anyone else noticed the updates icon has moved to behind a 3-dot menu when using the calendar view? It is super inconvenient because our workflow is dependent on team members easily knowing when there are updates on an item. Now they have to click through on every item which is super inconvenient and has already caused us to have to do double work.

I have noticed this and it has been ruining my life, not to be dramatic. I can’t see the notes my colleague has left for me. She thought I was ignoring her notes! I did an entire week’s worth of work thinking she didn’t leave me any direction.

posts.author: KSowerby (Kate)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I really dislike the new update
